lede: A look at December 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including L. L. Zamenhof and Gustave Eiffel, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: South Sudanese Civil War
events.desc: The South Sudanese Civil War begins when opposition leaders Dr. Riek Machar, Pagan Amum and Rebecca Nyandeng vote to boycott the meeting of the National Liberation Council at Nyakuron.

events.title: Venera 7
events.desc: Soviet spacecraft Venera 7 successfully lands on Venus. It is the first successful soft landing on another planet.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of Mount Austen, the Galloping Horse, and the Sea Horse begins during the Guadalcanal campaign.

events.title: Gone with the Wind (film)
events.desc: Gone with the Wind (highest inflation adjusted grossing film) receives its premiere at Loew's Grand Theatre in Atlanta, Georgia, United States.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Serbian Army recaptures Belgrade from the invading Austro-Hungarian Army.

events.title: Symphony No. 9 (Dvořák)
events.desc: Symphony No. 9 ("From the New World" a.k.a. the "New World Symphony") by Antonín Dvořák premieres in a public afternoon rehearsal at Carnegie Hall in New York City, followed by a concert premiere on the evening of December 16.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Battle of Fredericksburg ends in a Union defeat as General Ambrose Burnside withdraws the Army of the Potomac across the Rappahannock River.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British and French fleets clash in the Battle of St. Lucia.

events.title: Constantine VIII
events.desc: Constantine VIII becomes sole emperor of the Byzantine Empire, 63 years after being crowned co-emperor.

events.title: Pope Sergius I
events.desc: Pope Sergius I is elected as a compromise between antipopes Paschal and Theodore.

births.name: L. L. Zamenhof
births.line: Polish linguist and ophthalmologist, created Esperanto

births.name: Gustave Eiffel
births.line: French architect and engineer, co-designed the Eiffel Tower

births.name: Henri Becquerel
births.line: French physicist and chemist, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Uziel Gal
births.line: German-Israeli engineer, designed the Uzi gun

deaths.text: Walt Disney, American animator, director, producer, and screenwriter, co-founded The Walt Disney Company
Vallabhbhai Patel, Indian lawyer and politician, 1st Deputy Prime Minister of India

observances.text: Bill of Rights Day (United States) 2nd Amendment Day (South Carolina)
Christian feast day: John Horden and Robert McDonald (Episcopal Church (USA))
Christian feast day: Maria Crocifissa di Rosa
